methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Skyrora is a private-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $66M to date. The valuation landscape comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs. However, traditional triangulation methods face material constraints.

Peer-median analysis is not applicable: while 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, filtering by stage and valuation tier yielded fewer than 3 datapoints, making median-based estimates unreliable. Public-comp anchoring using the sector's 25.8x P/S median (median of 6 tickers) cannot proceed without Skyrora's disclosed revenue, which private companies typically do not publish. These methodological gaps reflect the structural opacity and heterogeneity of early-stage frontier tech, where funding stage, technology readiness, and business model vary widely within the same sector.

The analysis does not support a triangulated valuation range at this time. A defensible estimate would require either: (1) narrower private peer data with known valuations at comparable funding stages, (2) disclosed or modeled revenue for public-comp anchoring, or (3) forward-looking metrics specific to the company's market opportunity. Key uncertainties include development stage relative to peers, revenue trajectory, and customer traction—variables that typically drive 10x+ valuation spreads in frontier aerospace. Further diligence is warranted before formal price target derivation.
